name: Senator Patty Murray
subject: The Georgia runoff
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY PEOPLE FOR PATTY MURRAY
body:
#

Team,
This has been a long week, and while I'm hopeful we'll soon know for sure that Joe Biden and Kamala Harris are heading to the White House, I want to make sure we're also focused on the path to a Senate Democratic majority that runs through Georgia.
**Raphael Warnock and Jon Ossoff are both heading to a runoff election that could determine control of the Senate.**
**[Our work to elect a Democratic majority isn't finished, . Will you rush in a $5 contribution to stand with Raphael Warnock and Jon Ossoff in the Georgia runoff election?](https://act.pattymurray.?g=KnQfVcQot1BdNp6fCr3xM1A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pm-201106-georgia-runoff?refcode=em201106i_535&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=98208&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Everett&state=WA&phone=&employer=&occupation=")**

While our race in Kentucky may be over, we unfortunately still don't know who has control of the Senate, Peter. And, while it may be easier to just sit on the sidelines today, I've never backed down from a fight before and I REFUSE to start now. Peter, your action today will not just make or break the future of the Senate, but the future of our NATION. I wouldn't be asking if it wasn't important, but we've already come so far and I can't just sit by and give up our majority. I need your help today more than EVER before, Peter. We're not going to win, if you fail to take action today. Mitch McConnell Text MITCH to 47360 to receive mobile alerts! Team Mitch is happy to help at 1 800-403-1744. Reply HELP for help, STOP to end. Message frequency may vary. Msg&DataRatesMayApply. Terms and Conditions . name: Tom Fitton
subject: Election Update
body:
Judicial Watch Takes on FBI in Federal Court

[Advertisement]
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/wta/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=51983&L=21166&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cueW91dHViZS5jb20vd2F0Y2g/dj1RcWk1Q3Y0T0ZKZyZ1dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycyZ1dG1fY29udGVudD0yMDIwMTEwNzA0MTYzOA==]

[WEEKLY UPDATE]

ELECTION UPDATE

[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/wta/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=51983&L=21158&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cueW91dHViZS5jb20vd2F0Y2g/dj1hTTVrUkZWeGlRRSZ1dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycyZ1dG1fY29udGVudD0yMDIwMTEwNzA0MTYzOA==]

I take no comfort in saying that we saw this coming. Back in June,
for example, I told
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/wta/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=51983&L=21159&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cueW91dHViZS5jb20vd2F0Y2g/dj1lM3FfMlhMeklfdyZ1dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycyZ1dG1fY29udGVudD0yMDIwMTEwNzA0MTYzOA==]
Lou
Dobbs:

“It’s been a state by state battle to protect the integrity of our
elections. There will be a severe temptation to cheat, and now
that’s going to be a lot easier to do because our system is going to
be overwhelmed with ballots and applications for ballots.

We’ve proven repeatedly – because we’ve been winning in court to
clean up voter lists – that the lists across America are filthy in
terms of having more people on the rolls than are eligible to vote.
And those people, you can be sure, are going to be to be the groups
getting some of these ballots.
Our most recent research
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/wta/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=51983&L=20909&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvcHJlc3MtcmVsZWFzZXMvbmV3LWp3LXN0dWR5LXZvdGVyLXJlZ2lzdHJhdGlvbi8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnMmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9MjAyMDExMDcwNDE2Mzg=]
in September revealed that 353 U.S. counties had 1.8 million more
registered voters than eligible voting-age citizens. In other words,
the registration rates of those counties exceeded 100% of eligible
voters!

I have to wonder how much worse it might have been had we not been
busy in court. As you know, we are a national leader in seeking
cleaner elections.

In 2018, the Supreme Court upheld a voter roll cleanup program that
resulted from our settlement of a federal lawsuit with Ohio
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/wta/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=51983&L=17110&F=T&].
California settled
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/wta/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=51983&L=15011&F=T&]
a lawsuit with us and last year began the process of removing up to
1.6 million inactive names from Los Angeles County’s rolls. Kentucky
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/wta/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=51983&L=17111&F=T&]
also began a cleanup of hundreds of thousands of old registrations
last year after it entered into a consent decree to end another
Judicial Watch lawsuit. In September this year, we sued Illinois
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/wta/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=51983&L=20594&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvcHJlc3MtcmVsZWFzZXMvaWxsaW5vaXMtcmVmdXNlcy12b3Rlci1yb2xsLWRhdGEvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJzJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PTIwMjAxMTA3MDQxNjM4]
for refusing to disclose voter roll data in violation of federal law.

We just analyzed data in the key state of Nevada where we found that
in Clark County, there were about 154,000 “inactive” voters on the
election rolls just before Election Day. Speaking of Election Day,
federal law seems clear that the key results were supposed to be
decided on ... Election Day. Here is one of the relevant statues:

The electors of President and Vice President shall be appointed, in
each State, on the Tuesday next after the first Monday in November, in
every fourth year succeeding every election of a President and Vice
President. 3 U.S. Code § 1
Indeed, on Tuesday, the president had the votes to win the presidency.
These vote totals are being changed because of unprecedented,
extraordinary, illicitly secretive, and inherently suspect counting
AFTER Election Day that continue as I write. There will be court
battles (which will almost certainly involve your Judicial Watch) but
the presidency may ultimately come down to a fight in Congress over
the counting of Electoral College electors from the states that are
engaged in the slow-motion counting of votes. State legislatures in
Pennsylvania, Michigan, Wisconsin, Georgia (and maybe Arizona) will
have decisions to make – endorse and ratify what happened this week
to President Trump’s vote totals or appoint a slate of electors to
reelect the president. So, in addition to supporting Judicial
Watch’s effort to secure the election, you may want to share your
views on the election controversy with your elected representatives,
both in your statehouse and in Congress.

COURT ORDERED HEARING ON MCCABE EMAILS AND TEXTS

We were in federal court this week to fight the FBI’s delay in
producing emails and text messages sent and received by former Acting
Director of the FBI Andrew McCabe. Judge Tanya S. Chutkan presided.

This hearing came in our FOIA lawsuit
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/wta/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=51983&L=19894&F=T&]
on behalf of Jeffrey A. Danik, a retired FBI supervisory special
agent, for emails and text messages of McCabe about McCabe’s
“conflicts of interest” regarding his wife’s (Dr. Jill
McCabe’s) political campaign and Hillary Clinton (_Jeffrey A. Danik
v. U.S. Department of Justice_
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/wta/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=51983&L=4146&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnQtYXJjaGl2ZS9kYW5pay12LWRvai1jb21wbGFpbnQtcmVkYWN0ZWQtMDE3OTIvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJzJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PTIwMjAxMTA3MDQxNjM4]
(No. 1:17-cv-01792)).

We’re asking for:

Text messages and emails of McCabe containing “Dr. Jill McCabe,”
“Jill,” “Common Good VA,” “Terry McAuliffe,”
“Clinton,” “Virginia Democratic Party,” “Democrat,”
“Conflict,” “Senate,” “Virginia Senate,” “Until I
return,” “Paris,” “France,” “Campaign,” “Run,”
“Political,” “Wife,” “Donation,” “OGC,” Email,” or
“New York Times.”
Judge Chutkan ordered the hearing
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/wta/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=51983&L=21147&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2RhbmlrLXYtZG9qLW1jY2FiZS1jb21tcy1oZWFyaW5nLW9yZGVyLTAxNzkyLz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycyZ1dG1fY29udGVudD0yMDIwMTEwNzA0MTYzOA==]
after receiving a September 21 Joint Status Report

U.S. LOSES $19 BILLION IN AFGHANISTAN TO FRAUD, WASTE, ABUSE

On the campaign trail President Trump frequently mentioned that
we’ve been in Afghanistan for 19 years, and that’s enough. It’s
not just the lives of American troops that are at stake. It’s
taxpayer money, our _Corruption Chronicles_ blog reports
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/wta/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=51983&L=21160&F=T&

A staggering $19 billion spent by the U.S. government to reconstruct
Afghanistan has been lost to fraud, waste, and abuse in the last
decade and that only includes a portion of the money allocated by
Congress for the cause. In 90% of the cases examined in a new federal
audit, the money was blown “carelessly, extravagantly, or to no
purpose.” This month the Special Inspector General for Afghanistan
Reconstruction (SIGAR) published a troubling report
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/wta/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=51983&L=21161&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuc2lnYXIubWlsL3BkZi9zcGVjaWFscHJvamVjdHMvU0lHQVItMjEtMDUtU1AucGRmP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJzJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PTIwMjAxMTA3MDQxNjM4]
that
analyzes less than half of the U.S. budget for the initiative, $63
billion in projects. As of December 2019, Congress has appropriated
$134 billion for Afghanistan reconstruction, which began roughly a
year after the U.S. invaded the Islamic nation to destroy Al-Qaeda and
eradicate the Taliban.

Afghanistan reconstruction has been a huge and well-chronicled debacle
that continues fleecing American taxpayers nearly two decades after
its inception. Judicial Watch has reported on the various boondoggles
over the years, most of them documented in tremendous detail by the
SIGAR. Highlights include the mysterious disappearance
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/wta/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=51983&L=4061&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvYmxvZy8yMDEyLzA5L3Utcy1sb3Nlcy00NzUtbWlsLWluLW9pbC1mb3ItYWZnaGFuLWFybXkvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJzJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PTIwMjAxMTA3MDQxNjM4]
of nearly half a billion dollars in oil destined for the Afghan
National Army, a $335 million Afghan power plant
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/wta/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=51983&L=4062&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvYmxvZy8yMDE1LzA4LzMzNS1taWwtYWZnaGFuLXBvd2VyLXBsYW50LWZ1bmRlZC1ieS11LXMtcnVpbmVkLWR1ZS10by1sYWNrLW9mLXVzZS8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnMmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9MjAyMDExMDcwNDE2Mzg=]
that is seldom used and an $18.5 million
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/wta/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=51983&L=21162&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvYmxvZy8yMDE0LzExL2NsaW50b24tc3RhdGUtZGVwdC1ibGV3LTE4LTUtbWlsLXJlbm92YXRlLXVudXNlZC1hZmdoYW4tamFpbC8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnMmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9MjAyMDExMDcwNDE2Mzg=]
renovation for a prison that remains unfinished and unused years after
the U.S.-funded work began. Over the summer, the U.S. government got
slammed in an audit for spending tens of millions of dollars on
useless and ineffective drug addiction programs in Afghanistan as part
of the reconstruction effort. In a scathing report
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/wta/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=51983&L=21163&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuc2lnYXIubWlsL3BkZi9hdWRpdHMvU0lHQVItMTktNDktQVIucGRmP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJzJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PTIwMjAxMTA3MDQxNjM4],
the SIGAR
blasts the U.S. for not knowing the impact of its investment and
failing to conduct site visits to project locations or maintain
required files or records.

The latest probe is especially impactful because it takes a
comprehensive look at projects over a lengthier period. The 19-page
report is littered with a multitude of examples of waste that should
outrage every taxpaying American. For instance, books provided for an
education program have never been used because they are in poor
condition and an empty sports stadium that is unlikely to ever be used
because it was not designed correctly to host soccer games and does
not have a functioning irrigation system. Investigators also found
poor workmanship and a lack of maintenance, including falling ceiling
tiles, broken drainage grates, and unlevelled playing surfaces with
incomplete water systems protruding from the empty soccer field. A
$5.2 million Kang Border Patrol headquarters compound has never been
utilized and is not being maintained despite being allocated
maintenance funds. The outrageous list of examples goes on and on
throughout the lengthy document.

A section on fraud includes cases of crimes involving federal
procurement and contract fraud as well as theft, corruption, bribery
of government employees and public officials. The SIGAR identifies 30
instances of procurement and contract fraud with a total value of more
than $296 million. Examples include a translator with U.S. Special
Forces who started a trucking and logistics company that paid $140,000
in bribes and gratuities to U.S. service members to assist him in
obtaining work. In another instance a contractor committed millions of
dollars in fraud involving food service deals affecting the U.S.
Central Command. Investigators also exposed a ring in which
individuals were for years fraudulently selling U.S. Embassy Kabul
meal cards. This resulted in the U.S. government losing between
$50,000 and $80,000 monthly for a total of $3 million. Investigators
also found that in the last two years alone, more than $3 billion was
lost to fraud or corruption.

“Endemic corruption, widespread insecurity, and a lack of
accountability over on-budget assistance continue to make any
investments made in Afghanistan vulnerable to waste, fraud and abuse
and may threaten the peace process as well as the perceived legitimacy
and effectiveness of the Afghan government,” the new SIGAR report
states. On a positive note, the inspector general’s work has
resulted in about $3.2 billion in savings for the U.S. taxpayer.
“Based on our audit recommendations, we identified approximately
half of this savings, or $1.6 billion, as funds that agencies, such as
the DOD or the Department of State (State), could put to better use
for other programs or efforts,” according to the watchdog.

MAYOR BLAMES VIRUS FOR NEW YORK’S CRISIS OF CRIME & DISORDER

Watching the leftist politicians play politics with our health and
shirk their responsibility for controlling violence has been quite an
eye opener. Micah Morrison, our chief investigative reporter, looks
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/wta/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=51983&L=21164&F=T&]
at one of most controversial leftist politicians in the country in our
_Investigative Bulletin_:

The presidential race is rocketing toward the finish line. But it’s
not the only game in town. In New York City, Left and Right alike are
eyeing the end of Mayor Bill de Blasio’s wretched tenure. The stakes
are high.

A year ago—long before Covid-19 hit the U.S.—Judicial Watch warned
that New York was slipping toward a crisis of crime and disorder.
Statistics told part of the story. Shootings, up; gun arrests, up;
hate crimes, up. A resurgent left-wing political class was about to
unleash new laws
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/wta/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=51983&L=15946&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvaW52ZXN0aWdhdGl2ZS1idWxsZXRpbi90aGUtbGVmdC1sb3Nlcy1pdHMtbWluZC1pbi1qYWlscy1kZWJhdGUvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJzJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PTIwMjAxMTA3MDQxNjM4]
that would put more felons on the streets. Quality of life was
unraveling. “Throughout the city,” we noted
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/wta/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=51983&L=15594&F=T&],
“anecdotal reports of unsettling incidents—harassment, menacing,
petty theft, public urination, graffiti, dope smokers, turnstile
jumpers—are increasing.”

Mayor de Blasio at the time was trampling through the fields of Iowa
in a delusional quest
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/wta/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=51983&L=21063&F=T&]
for the Democratic Party’s presidential nomination. Dismissive of
concerns back home and courting the Left, he promised he would keep
the city in “the vanguard of progressivism” and “improve the
quality of life of everyday New Yorkers.”

What a difference a year makes. Term-limited out of office in 2021, de
Blasio today is a ghost of his former self, a fading force in local
politics, seemingly broken and bewildered by the city. His pledge to
improve the quality of life of New Yorkers is in ruins.

These days, no one disputes New York is in crisis. Covid-19 is only
part of the story. The mayor was slow to take the virus seriously
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/wta/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=51983&L=21064&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubnl0aW1lcy5jb20vMjAyMC8wMy8xNi9ueXJlZ2lvbi9jb3JvbmF2aXJ1cy1iaWxsLWRlLWJsYXNpby5odG1sP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJzJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PTIwMjAxMTA3MDQxNjM4],
delayed closing restaurants and schools, and engaged in a long-running
feud with Governor Andrew Cuomo that may have cost thousands of lives.
Read ProPublica’s report on that devastating debacle here
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/wta/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=51983&L=21065&F=T&].

Cuomo appears to have steadied the state, keeping infection rates low.
But the mayor continues to steer an erratic course and crime continues
to rise. According to NYPD statistics, murders in New York City are up
40% from last year. Shootings are up 91%.

In August, there were 242 shootings in the city, up from 91 in the
same month last year. In September, 152 shootings, compared to 67 last
year.

Murders increased 79% in September compared to a year earlier—51
dead in September 2020, 29 in 2019.

Crime on New York’s subways—an economic lifeline of the city—is
increasing. New Yorkers averaged more than 5 million trips on weekdays
before the pandemic. Those numbers have plunged by more than 60% and
train travel has grown more dangerous. “So far this year,” says
the New York Times, “the number of reported homicides, rapes,
burglaries and robberies in the subway are higher than the same period
last year, according to Police Department statistics. Incidents of
vandalism have also spiked.”

The mayor blames all this on the pandemic. “We saw the violence
start in earnest in May and into June, into July,” de Blasio
recently said. “The original cause of this is all the effects of the
coronavirus.”

That’s clearly not the case, as statistics from 2019 and early 2020
show. The Left’s failed experiment with criminal justice reform, an
effort to empty jails that put repeat offenders back on the streets,
is another factor in the city’s decline. As Judicial Watch reported
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/wta/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=51983&L=17990&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvaW52ZXN0aWdhdGl2ZS1idWxsZXRpbi9wcm9ncmVzc2l2ZS1wb2xpY3ktZHJpdmVzLXJpc2luZy1jcmltZS1pbi1uZXcteW9yay8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnMmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9MjAyMDExMDcwNDE2Mzg=]
in March, before the pandemic hit, progressive forces rammed through
the state legislature an insane reform package that eliminated cash
bail for a wide range of offenses
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/wta/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=51983&L=17971&F=T&
assault, arson and child abuse, to manslaughter, robbery, and
riots—and removed judicial discretion in holding suspects.

The reform legislation took effect on January 1 and crime rates
jumped. Numbers for January and February of 2020 show a 35 percent
increase in robberies and a 64 percent increase in stolen cars
compared to the same period in 2019, the New York Post. Shootings went
up 19 percent. Burglaries went up 21 percent.

Public outrage was swift and legislators quickly backtracked. The
reform package was overhauled
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/wta/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=51983&L=21066&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly9nb3RoYW1pc3QuY29tL25ld3Mvd2hhdC1uZXctcm9sbGJhY2tzLWJhaWwtcmVmb3JtLW1lYW4tbmV3LXlvcms/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnMmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9MjAyMDExMDcwNDE2Mzg=]
to tighten restrictions on violent crimes and repeat offenders, but
the new iteration is seen as piecemeal by both sides. The fight over
emptying New York’s jails is not over.

Other blows hit the city before the pandemic as well. In education
policy—New York’s school system is the largest in the country,
with 1.1 million students and a budget of $34 billion—de Blasio
plunged the city into turmoil with the appointment of a radical
chancellor, Richard Carranza. A tireless class warrior
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/wta/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=51983&L=21067&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly9ueXBvc3QuY29tLzIwMTkvMDUvMjgvcmljaGFyZC1jYXJyYW56YXMtcHJlanVkaWNpYWwtcmFjZS1wb2xpdGljcy1oYXZlLWdvbmUtdG9vLWZhci8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnMmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9MjAyMDExMDcwNDE2Mzg=],
Carranza has attempted to ram through a left-wing agenda under the
banner of “diversity,” seeking to impose racial quotas, eliminate
programs for gifted students, and undermine admission standards to
elite high schools.

I recently wrote an article for the New York Post about my experiences
with the Carranza mob. Read it here
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/wta/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=51983&L=21068&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly9ueXBvc3QuY29tLzIwMjAvMDkvMjIvZGUtYmxhc2lvLWNhcnJhbnphLWRpdmVyc2l0eS1kcml2ZS1pcy13cm9uZy1hbmQtbG9va3MtY29ycnVwdC8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnMmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9MjAyMDExMDcwNDE2Mzg=].

And in another deeply worrying sign of trouble, New York’s economic
picture is grim. Job losses are dragging the city down. “More than
one million residents are out of work,” reports
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/wta/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=51983&L=21069&F=T&]
the New York Times, “and the unemployment rate is nearly double the
national average.”

But the Left presses on with an anti-growth agenda. Last month,
progressive forces in the New York City Council killed a waterfront
development project, Industry City, that would have created 20,000
jobs. The reason? Opposition to “big business” from environmental
and community groups. Establishment Democrats, terrified of rising
progressive forces in the party, jumped on board when it became clear
Industry City was going down. Read more about it here
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/wta/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=51983&L=21070&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubnl0aW1lcy5jb20vMjAyMC8wOS8yMy9ueXJlZ2lvbi9pbmR1c3RyeS1jaXR5LXJlem9uaW5nLW55Yy5odG1sP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJzJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PTIwMjAxMTA3MDQxNjM4].

If Industry City sounds depressingly familiar, it’s because the same
thing happened a year ago in Queens. Progressive forces led by
Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez killed a development deal with Amazon. The
tech giant was set to bring 25,000 jobs to the city. Ocasio-Cortez
hailed the death of the deal as a victory against “corporate greed.

The race to succeed de Blasio already is underway and his would-be
successors in Democrat-dominated New York are all tacking left, hoping
for a repeat
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/wta/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=51983&L=21071&F=T&]
of recent progressive victories. As New York goes, they know, so goes
much of urban America. The mayoralty of New York City is one of the
biggest prizes in American politics. Radical Democrats are determined
to seize it.

Until next week,

clean_body: Judicial Watch Takes on FBI in Federal Court [Advertisement] [==] [WEEKLY UPDATE] ELECTION UPDATE [==] I take no comfort in saying that we saw this coming. Back in June, for example, I told [==] Lou Dobbs: “It’s been a state by state battle to protect the integrity of our elections. There will be a severe temptation to cheat, and now that’s going to be a lot easier to do because our system is going to be overwhelmed with ballots and applications for ballots. We’ve proven repeatedly – because we’ve been winning in court to clean up voter lists – that the lists across America are filthy in terms of having more people on the rolls than are eligible to vote. And those people, you can be sure, are going to be to be the groups getting some of these ballots. Our most recent research [=] in September revealed that 353 U.S. counties had 1.8 million more registered voters than eligible voting-age citizens. In other words, the registration rates of those counties exceeded 100% of eligible voters! I have to wonder how much worse it might have been had we not been busy in court. As you know, we are a national leader in seeking cleaner elections. In 2018, the Supreme Court upheld a voter roll cleanup program that resulted from our settlement of a federal lawsuit with Ohio [=]. California settled [=] a lawsuit with us and last year began the process of removing up to 1.6 million inactive names from Los Angeles County’s rolls. Kentucky [==] also began a cleanup of hundreds of thousands of old registrations last year after it entered into a consent decree to end another Judicial Watch lawsuit. In September this year, we sued Illinois [] for refusing to disclose voter roll data in violation of federal law. We just analyzed data in the key state of Nevada where we found that in Clark County, there were about 154,000 “inactive” voters on the election rolls just before Election Day. Speaking of Election Day, federal law seems clear that the key results were supposed to be decided on ... Election Day. Here is one of the relevant statues: The electors of President and Vice President shall be appointed, in each State, on the Tuesday next after the first Monday in November, in every fourth year succeeding every election of a President and Vice President. 3 U.S. Code § 1 Indeed, on Tuesday, the president had the votes to win the presidency. These vote totals are being changed because of unprecedented, extraordinary, illicitly secretive, and inherently suspect counting AFTER Election Day that continue as I write. There will be court battles (which will almost certainly involve your Judicial Watch) but the presidency may ultimately come down to a fight in Congress over the counting of Electoral College electors from the states that are engaged in the slow-motion counting of votes. State legislatures in Pennsylvania, Michigan, Wisconsin, Georgia (and maybe Arizona) will have decisions to make – endorse and ratify what happened this week to President Trump’s vote totals or appoint a slate of electors to reelect the president. So, in addition to supporting Judicial Watch’s effort to secure the election, you may want to share your views on the election controversy with your elected representatives, both in your statehouse and in Congress. COURT ORDERED HEARING ON MCCABE EMAILS AND TEXTS We were in federal court this week to fight the FBI’s delay in producing emails and text messages sent and received by former Acting Director of the FBI Andrew McCabe. Judge Tanya S. Chutkan presided. This hearing came in our FOIA lawsuit [==] on behalf of Jeffrey A. Danik, a retired FBI supervisory special agent, for emails and text messages of McCabe about McCabe’s “conflicts of interest” regarding his wife’s (Dr. Jill McCabe’s) political campaign and Hillary Clinton (Jeffrey A. Danik v. U.S. Department of Justice [] (No. 1:17-cv-01792)). We’re asking for: Text messages and emails of McCabe containing “Dr. Jill McCabe,” “Jill,” “Common Good VA,” “Terry McAuliffe,” “Clinton,” “Virginia Democratic Party,” “Democrat,” “Conflict,” “Senate,” “Virginia Senate,” “Until I return,” “Paris,” “France,” “Campaign,” “Run,” “Political,” “Wife,” “Donation,” “OGC,” Email,” or “New York Times.” Judge Chutkan ordered the hearing [==] after receiving a September 21 Joint Status Report [==] from the parties in which we told the court: [Danik] is tired of waiting. The Court ruled almost four months ago, on May 31, 2020, that [Justice Department] failed to satisfy its burdens under FOIA. We however are no closer to a resolution of this case. [Danik] already substantially narrowed his request. He also does not believe 11 pages of text messages and 2,362 emails is too onerous to review. [Danik] believes he has waited long enough – almost four years since he sent his FOIA requests – for the agency to satisfy its burdens and produce all non-exempt, responsive records to him. We’re asking that the Justice Department release at least 750 pages of emails each month until completion. The FBI recently turned over several pages [] of almost completely redacted McCabe text messages while still denying the texts are subject to FOIA. In September 2020, we uncovered [=] in a separate lawsuit that senior members of Robert Mueller’s Special Counsel’s Office (SCO) repeatedly and “accidentally” wiped over 20 phones assigned to them. In 2017, we uncovered [] Justice Department records concerning ethics issues related to McCabe’s involvement with his wife’s political campaign. The documents include an email showing Mrs. McCabe was recruited for a Virginia state senate race in February 2015 [==] by then-Virginia Lieutenant Governor Ralph Northam’s office. The news that former Secretary of State Clinton used a private email server broke five days later [=], on March 2, 2015. Five days after that, former Clinton Foundation board member [] and Democrat party fundraiser, Virginia Governor Terry McAuliffe, met with the McCabes. She announced her candidacy on March 12. Soon afterward, Clinton/McAuliffe-aligned political groups donated nearly [==] $700,000 (40% [] of the campaign’s total funds) to McCabe’s wife for her campaign. McCabe was fired from the FBI in March 2018 for leaking to the media and lacking “candor [= I was able to listen in on the hearing and it seemed that the court was unhappy with the slow pace of the FBI’s production and demanded more information from the agency about the delays. We will see what the FBI comes up with, but you can see how Judicial Watch’s legal fights for transparency will continue no matter who is president. U.S. LOSES $19 BILLION IN AFGHANISTAN TO FRAUD, WASTE, ABUSE On the campaign trail President Trump frequently mentioned that we’ve been in Afghanistan for 19 years, and that’s enough. It’s not just the lives of American troops that are at stake. It’s taxpayer money, our Corruption Chronicles blog reports [ A staggering $19 billion spent by the U.S. government to reconstruct Afghanistan has been lost to fraud, waste, and abuse in the last decade and that only includes a portion of the money allocated by Congress for the cause. In 90% of the cases examined in a new federal audit, the money was blown “carelessly, extravagantly, or to no purpose.” This month the Special Inspector General for Afghanistan Reconstruction (SIGAR) published a troubling report [] that analyzes less than half of the U.S. budget for the initiative, $63 billion in projects. As of December 2019, Congress has appropriated $134 billion for Afghanistan reconstruction, which began roughly a year after the U.S. invaded the Islamic nation to destroy Al-Qaeda and eradicate the Taliban. Afghanistan reconstruction has been a huge and well-chronicled debacle that continues fleecing American taxpayers nearly two decades after its inception. Judicial Watch has reported on the various boondoggles over the years, most of them documented in tremendous detail by the SIGAR. Highlights include the mysterious disappearance [] of nearly half a billion dollars in oil destined for the Afghan National Army, a $335 million Afghan power plant [=] that is seldom used and an $18.5 million [=] renovation for a prison that remains unfinished and unused years after the U.S.-funded work began. Over the summer, the U.S. government got slammed in an audit for spending tens of millions of dollars on useless and ineffective drug addiction programs in Afghanistan as part of the reconstruction effort. In a scathing report [], the SIGAR blasts the U.S. for not knowing the impact of its investment and failing to conduct site visits to project locations or maintain required files or records. The latest probe is especially impactful because it takes a comprehensive look at projects over a lengthier period. The 19-page report is littered with a multitude of examples of waste that should outrage every taxpaying American. For instance, books provided for an education program have never been used because they are in poor condition and an empty sports stadium that is unlikely to ever be used because it was not designed correctly to host soccer games and does not have a functioning irrigation system. Investigators also found poor workmanship and a lack of maintenance, including falling ceiling tiles, broken drainage grates, and unlevelled playing surfaces with incomplete water systems protruding from the empty soccer field. A $5.2 million Kang Border Patrol headquarters compound has never been utilized and is not being maintained despite being allocated maintenance funds. The outrageous list of examples goes on and on throughout the lengthy document. A section on fraud includes cases of crimes involving federal procurement and contract fraud as well as theft, corruption, bribery of government employees and public officials. The SIGAR identifies 30 instances of procurement and contract fraud with a total value of more than $296 million. Examples include a translator with U.S. Special Forces who started a trucking and logistics company that paid $140,000 in bribes and gratuities to U.S. service members to assist him in obtaining work. In another instance a contractor committed millions of dollars in fraud involving food service deals affecting the U.S. Central Command. Investigators also exposed a ring in which individuals were for years fraudulently selling U.S. Embassy Kabul meal cards. This resulted in the U.S. government losing between $50,000 and $80,000 monthly for a total of $3 million. Investigators also found that in the last two years alone, more than $3 billion was lost to fraud or corruption. “Endemic corruption, widespread insecurity, and a lack of accountability over on-budget assistance continue to make any investments made in Afghanistan vulnerable to waste, fraud and abuse and may threaten the peace process as well as the perceived legitimacy and effectiveness of the Afghan government,” the new SIGAR report states. On a positive note, the inspector general’s work has resulted in about $3.2 billion in savings for the U.S. taxpayer. “Based on our audit recommendations, we identified approximately half of this savings, or $1.6 billion, as funds that agencies, such as the DOD or the Department of State (State), could put to better use for other programs or efforts,” according to the watchdog. MAYOR BLAMES VIRUS FOR NEW YORK’S CRISIS OF CRIME & DISORDER Watching the leftist politicians play politics with our health and shirk their responsibility for controlling violence has been quite an eye opener. Micah Morrison, our chief investigative reporter, looks [] at one of most controversial leftist politicians in the country in our Investigative Bulletin: The presidential race is rocketing toward the finish line. But it’s not the only game in town. In New York City, Left and Right alike are eyeing the end of Mayor Bill de Blasio’s wretched tenure. The stakes are high. A year ago—long before Covid-19 hit the U.S.—Judicial Watch warned that New York was slipping toward a crisis of crime and disorder. Statistics told part of the story. Shootings, up; gun arrests, up; hate crimes, up. A resurgent left-wing political class was about to unleash new laws [] that would put more felons on the streets. Quality of life was unraveling. “Throughout the city,” we noted [=], “anecdotal reports of unsettling incidents—harassment, menacing, petty theft, public urination, graffiti, dope smokers, turnstile jumpers—are increasing.” Mayor de Blasio at the time was trampling through the fields of Iowa in a delusional quest [] for the Democratic Party’s presidential nomination. Dismissive of concerns back home and courting the Left, he promised he would keep the city in “the vanguard of progressivism” and “improve the quality of life of everyday New Yorkers.” What a difference a year makes. Term-limited out of office in 2021, de Blasio today is a ghost of his former self, a fading force in local politics, seemingly broken and bewildered by the city. His pledge to improve the quality of life of New Yorkers is in ruins. These days, no one disputes New York is in crisis. Covid-19 is only part of the story. The mayor was slow to take the virus seriously [], delayed closing restaurants and schools, and engaged in a long-running feud with Governor Andrew Cuomo that may have cost thousands of lives. Read ProPublica’s report on that devastating debacle here [=]. Cuomo appears to have steadied the state, keeping infection rates low. But the mayor continues to steer an erratic course and crime continues to rise. According to NYPD statistics, murders in New York City are up 40% from last year. Shootings are up 91%. In August, there were 242 shootings in the city, up from 91 in the same month last year. In September, 152 shootings, compared to 67 last year. Murders increased 79% in September compared to a year earlier—51 dead in September 2020, 29 in 2019. Crime on New York’s subways—an economic lifeline of the city—is increasing. New Yorkers averaged more than 5 million trips on weekdays before the pandemic. Those numbers have plunged by more than 60% and train travel has grown more dangerous. “So far this year,” says the New York Times, “the number of reported homicides, rapes, burglaries and robberies in the subway are higher than the same period last year, according to Police Department statistics. Incidents of vandalism have also spiked.” The mayor blames all this on the pandemic. “We saw the violence start in earnest in May and into June, into July,” de Blasio recently said. “The original cause of this is all the effects of the coronavirus.” That’s clearly not the case, as statistics from 2019 and early 2020 show. The Left’s failed experiment with criminal justice reform, an effort to empty jails that put repeat offenders back on the streets, is another factor in the city’s decline. As Judicial Watch reported [=] in March, before the pandemic hit, progressive forces rammed through the state legislature an insane reform package that eliminated cash bail for a wide range of offenses [== assault, arson and child abuse, to manslaughter, robbery, and riots—and removed judicial discretion in holding suspects. The reform legislation took effect on January 1 and crime rates jumped. Numbers for January and February of 2020 show a 35 percent increase in robberies and a 64 percent increase in stolen cars compared to the same period in 2019, the New York Post. Shootings went up 19 percent. Burglaries went up 21 percent. Public outrage was swift and legislators quickly backtracked. The reform package was overhauled [=] to tighten restrictions on violent crimes and repeat offenders, but the new iteration is seen as piecemeal by both sides. The fight over emptying New York’s jails is not over. Other blows hit the city before the pandemic as well. In education policy—New York’s school system is the largest in the country, with 1.1 million students and a budget of $34 billion—de Blasio plunged the city into turmoil with the appointment of a radical chancellor, Richard Carranza. A tireless class warrior [=], Carranza has attempted to ram through a left-wing agenda under the banner of “diversity,” seeking to impose racial quotas, eliminate programs for gifted students, and undermine admission standards to elite high schools. I recently wrote an article for the New York Post about my experiences with the Carranza mob. Read it here [=]. And in another deeply worrying sign of trouble, New York’s economic picture is grim. Job losses are dragging the city down. “More than one million residents are out of work,” reports [=] the New York Times, “and the unemployment rate is nearly double the national average.” But the Left presses on with an anti-growth agenda. Last month, progressive forces in the New York City Council killed a waterfront development project, Industry City, that would have created 20,000 jobs. The reason? Opposition to “big business” from environmental and community groups. Establishment Democrats, terrified of rising progressive forces in the party, jumped on board when it became clear Industry City was going down. Read more about it here []. If Industry City sounds depressingly familiar, it’s because the same thing happened a year ago in Queens. Progressive forces led by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez killed a development deal with Amazon. The tech giant was set to bring 25,000 jobs to the city. Ocasio-Cortez hailed the death of the deal as a victory against “corporate greed. The race to succeed de Blasio already is underway and his would-be successors in Democrat-dominated New York are all tacking left, hoping for a repeat [=] of recent progressive victories. As New York goes, they know, so goes much of urban America. The mayoralty of New York City is one of the biggest prizes in American politics. Radical Democrats are determined to seize it. name: Bill Hagerty
subject: The President needs YOUR HELP!
body:
President Trump is going to fight to protect the integrity of this election and fight to win it
Peter,President Trump is going to fight to protect the integrity of this election and fight to win it--the stakes of this election are enormous--we're going to make sure the results are fair and legal.I've donated to the President's efforts to defend our election and I hope you'll join me. [DEFEND THE INTEGRITY OF OUR ELECTION](https://hfs.cmail20.com/t/t-l-xiihuhk-fuutjhhtj-r/) Every vote that was cast legally should be counted, but I hope that states that are counting don't shift from counting ballots to casting ballots.That's why the President needs YOUR HELP![**Please donate and protect the integrity of our election.**](https://hfs.cmail20.com/t/t-l-xiihuhk-fuutjhhtj-y/) Bill Hagerty
U.S. Senator-elect (R-TN) | [](https://hfs.cmail20.com/t/t-l-xiihuhk-fuutjhhtj-j/)| [](https://hfs.cmail20.com/t/t-l-xiihuhk-fuutjhhtj-t/)| [](https://hfs.cmail20.com/t/t-l-xiihuhk-fuutjhhtj-i/)| [](https://hfs.cmail20.com/t/t-l-xiihuhk-fuutjhhtj-d/)

body:
---
| | | | | |
---
| FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE SATURDAY, NOVEMBER 7, 2020
ICYMI: RAPHAEL WARNOCK'S ANTI-ISRAEL RECORD EXPOSED
ATLANTA - In case you missed it, several news outlets recently exposed Reverend Raphael Warnock for his history of anti-Israel rhetoric - including a 2019 letter he signed comparing the Nation of Israel to "oppressive regimes" and the West Bank to "apartheid South Africa." Warnock has a long history of anti-Israel statements and positions, including repeatedly [defending](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001-y7-CSeq1WDSZbJEP0xOkNJv68LNkQH-S07KhTCIBaNoVjUmOxMtUJFXyMdbDz9w59yMy5jt6gJLBq2eORS5LD02hQPZ2lfxJankoH9YFbqaPpXFAvSTdN2UsS-NzSY266xrKluy0rmXyXo2NrSiE8HHml5uWBl_2pvZYHi--xbM162coISTuin6Rcsk0h2WEUadZD8M0sRaYWa-BjLiEnx0KOcsJQz7qLI2GXrN5qTSJPzDktLxeJYn_Pe9S5tB&c=cqYQZi2l-DpKeI6d702Y7Vt7ZJhIumPZpppM4goHU7ndPogabhQl2Q==&ch=RPZiWLGtOTOA2Y_zw3ozpbh0GRA1Kdo_DgymGFiM4-ermBiM2eCd8g==) Jeremiah Wright's anti-Semitic rhetoric and [endorsing](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001-y7-CSeq1WDSZbJEP0xOkNJv68LNkQH-S07KhTCIBaNoVjUmOxMtUJFXyMdbDz9wxZu6hWJVDPDEbVtqoy8r9gZhRb6I4VKCv0Lq0eC_fvfELCoXb5al8Tzsrr1YiQqN5eCbIIvonTbK_FQ3WF6TTUMceLeScCEJ0lYCNajwM695nHGFRi5rQFmRK2MO4Gq9Jh60ncFtx_P7WDT5YTn5vT7RyJ1dL7z2QoYzTqEMEXIwLWDEkw2oVyYYWud0yMf71qtqeYwsmYlVlCw2r_SqJY0751Y8kggBKsMqyoBUR3Y=&c=cqYQZi2l-DpKeI6d702Y7Vt7ZJhIumPZpppM4goHU7ndPogabhQl2Q==&ch=RPZiWLGtOTOA2Y_zw3ozpbh0GRA1Kdo_DgymGFiM4-ermBiM2eCd8g==) the BLM political organization, which strongly [supports](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001-y7-CSeq1WDSZbJEP0xOkNJv68LNkQH-S07KhTCIBaNoVjUmOxMtUJFXyMdbDz9wlAtUTaxgp9M5Sl8FYNMwEbe6B2eM9JA7k5DCU_2Js09NF03C2BAyozqt3TVr3tE3zj4rIG6Z6b3MOGZ0fXpWy3sRkac1hbSQcZ8-cQWyqx8CDGQSqrJme2mBQWIoqziSuBZjX8k4CmP4WwirOdZaHespkxVMZUvXU7rFo_uMHPG0g8SICv2PsomBmnfTOtmhOzTt6f48Ak1qolAuLT3yiOqRDXpgt0lV&c=cqYQZi2l-DpKeI6d702Y7Vt7ZJhIumPZpppM4goHU7ndPogabhQl2Q==&ch=RPZiWLGtOTOA2Y_zw3ozpbh0GRA1Kdo_DgymGFiM4-ermBiM2eCd8g==) the anti-Zionist Boycott, Divest, and Sanction (BDS) Movement.
[Fox News](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001-y7-CSeq1WDSZbJEP0xOkNJv68LNkQH-S07KhTCIBaNoVjUmOxMtUJFXyMdbDz9wsJcUek10MwEPd3I7AV8wp0ectm5aGZKFrzRktMEN6TtpMDh5W0I6f6YnG0iLmQ7wegQEKaD3LU4Xw9DidxcDDQQdvPXxNhLclmj7gvWfoC6ZSdeTPAGZhozz7w3s-Xr1mH_4Fc26nWaR7kQmKrGr4I5LiAK_UiOGaiaeDjUqDOpLOSvqxuGzsTTPzLm2_6LIoETvfdc8_fNjlUZKVB4qJQ==&c=cqYQZi2l-DpKeI6d702Y7Vt7ZJhIumPZpppM4goHU7ndPogabhQl2Q==&ch=RPZiWLGtOTOA2Y_zw3ozpbh0GRA1Kdo_DgymGFiM4-ermBiM2eCd8g==): "Georgia Dem Senate candidate signed letter comparing Israeli control of West Bank to apartheid South Africa"
[Jewish Insider](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001-y7-CSeq1WDSZbJEP0xOkNJv68LNkQH-S07KhTCIBaNoVjUmOxMtUJFXyMdbDz9w5By3P00QmL74D1fBbSjtkgskd0OVIgTPXM2_Fbu_BRGd7uxPXdi_pP3ocQge_ocG505GJ4Tf35DKmoLADWVjiZ6I5bI6CynFBkDb9fcRiVgwqi3KoOBh4wn5UJpdtKSjKtZPbWxXlbU=&c=cqYQZi2l-DpKeI6d702Y7Vt7ZJhIumPZpppM4goHU7ndPogabhQl2Q==&ch=RPZiWLGtOTOA2Y_zw3ozpbh0GRA1Kdo_DgymGFiM4-ermBiM2eCd8g==): "Raphael Warnock signed letter likening West Bank to apartheid South Africa"
[Jewish News Syndicate](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001-y7-CSeq1WDSZbJEP0xOkNJv68LNkQH-S07KhTCIBaNoVjUmOxMtUJFXyMdbDz9w_uXG-LaeNNUFsdp958tU_pLl_Jr39cjnHk732G9YcXa3GoekjwVHf9x-ZRyWjUnHodEjeSkpcEwbSjqQ37-3E1MJWgQeQnH-CjfRdXNl89IYEXAqNvi4hdT3Ck6SFJ8u_60DxkEyy2JbAhY2Eu6KtaGEm4_NQRV9V4r5mcdv_wY42ICFf-9SLA==&c=cqYQZi2l-DpKeI6d702Y7Vt7ZJhIumPZpppM4goHU7ndPogabhQl2Q==&ch=RPZiWLGtOTOA2Y_zw3ozpbh0GRA1Kdo_DgymGFiM4-ermBiM2eCd8g==): "GA's Warnock signed letter likening Israel's policies on Palestinians to apartheid"
[Jewish Journal](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001-y7-CSeq1WDSZbJEP0xOkNJv68LNkQH-S07KhTCIBaNoVjUmOxMtUJFXyMdbDz9wtkIs-1uYNDX_a34uzrKN7CoCIkmzWOIagn2THw3rRw-cABQ_9zasoG2eFzP505ZjEKjlJF1e42En6R5cqR1awSQX4NUk0mZuJCUWS89eCbsbKV466FONXe3wZvecAO4OFabUJIATotzVsS65mzTgwN6NwHfg8MzPqBFTyVUTz02A74ndmr8NKMVqjmtLlpMfY4gVnSEBEKU=&c=cqYQZi2l-DpKeI6d702Y7Vt7ZJhIumPZpppM4goHU7ndPogabhQl2Q==&ch=RPZiWLGtOTOA2Y_zw3ozpbh0GRA1Kdo_DgymGFiM4-ermBiM2eCd8g==): "Senate Candidate Warnock Signed Letter Accusing Israel of Apartheid"
[Washington Free Beacon](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001-y7-CSeq1WDSZbJEP0xOkNJv68LNkQH-S07KhTCIBaNoVjUmOxMtUJFXyMdbDz9w0BDQoqAQ4a0EDbECLJ1upHfBq71z-djhhAGoq9Mauj8frewLokGGMQ48Yao_s0xffX2pyVapqegnGc2gwUgyxyKVF9B59-M0PfgCXZQSFW_e67ML1JLafaVz91lnMeeSEosYna6S42ufJAmjjeQMamuDOfylvruOMvirg0Nq3FuOcSrDrepqanpnD27RO9hNBcOw-E5dh7-ovGOk_NOIHaVKbrqoD5K5&c=cqYQZi2l-DpKeI6d702Y7Vt7ZJhIumPZpppM4goHU7ndPogabhQl2Q==&ch=RPZiWLGtOTOA2Y_zw3ozpbh0GRA1Kdo_DgymGFiM4-ermBiM2eCd8g==): "Georgia Dem Senate Candidate Tied to Anti-Israel Letter Comparing West Bank to Apartheid"

Friend, this seat is one of them.

While votes are still being counted, we feel good about what we're seeing. We're confident we have the momentum to win and flip this seat RED -- taking back a seat that was only recently flipped blue in 2018 thanks to Bloomberg's billions.

But we're also confident that Nancy Pelosi will do whatever it takes to drag this out in court. She's got an army of lawyers behind her prepared to fight from every angle until the bitter end.

That's why we're coming to you. A legal battle could take weeks and cost a pretty penny. We're confident we have the momentum to win and flip this seat RED -- taking back a seat that was only recently flipped blue in 2018 thanks to Bloomberg's billions. . == == Friend, this seat is one of them. But we're also confident that Nancy Pelosi will do whatever it takes to drag this out in court. She's got an army of lawyers behind her prepared to fight from every angle until the bitter end. body:
We can't throw in the towel
Friend,
I just got out of a meeting with the rest of the Republican State finance team, and I wanted to reach out to you before we're forced to make some tough budget decisions.
My team is working around the clock to make sure we have the funds needed to continue defending State Republicans as they face critical recounts and runoff elections, but if we don't get a surge of donations right now, there's no way that we'll be able to be successful in our efforts.

What is the Republican State Leadership Committee (RSLC)?
We are the only organization solely focused on electing Republicans to down-ballot, state-level offices. We accomplish this by working directly with Republican leadership in the states and providing them with resources, training, and funding. We have nearly 200,000 donors in all 50 states. The RSLC invested a record $45 million into state-level races in the 2017-2018 election cycle, which contributed to state-level Republicans maintaining 61 of 99 legislative chamber majorities, holding 25 lieutenant governor seats, and 29 secretaries of state offices. Your support is critical to making this happen. You can learn more about us at RSLC.GOP.

name: Josh Hawley
subject: We Need Election Integrity NOW!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Josh Hawley for Senate
body:
[ ](http://email.hawleyformo.com/u/click?_t=11a592fdc01846d4a1c728421295853d&_m=89b16abf81c642adb83db914d85d9f09&_e=u2n3XGDy_PyS-9ncrWxAAngjq3lcfqs1elTHauK24GF0tVtwCJepntyxjZhwUGB0IO-hLpQR9VEaMEPJF0DMIrka_0upf-oafBPg3P_YQZExRosTfzBb_3W9jsmhWzdM0cqhy7G8wW3eJao2PMeSHf3dM-W73cmwhZR-tgACaXyflHe7rCeqx12yneh0bMexJnhxiiqcTpdmT7gU09Vr66ngxA9U7LGnYKViuRBhTLajCc4jk7bWrpQo0WcmB4pVKx9WgLZNj2bg_RD9jDL3_L6VfgM22P38GxziWz9W0NZoBBf4mc-byhG6zgrYRfHh7DMyqHbxkwC6LFfZM3pjaspwqbTLlmpTvr8TE7MpS8U%3D) | |
---
Peter,
**If the 2020 elections made anything clear, it’s that _greater accountability in our ballot-counting process is long past due._**

On Thursday night, I appeared on Fox News to discuss this critically important issue with Tucker Carlson.

_**If you missed my interview, check it out by clicking on the image below. **_
[ ](http://email.hawleyformo.com/u/click?_t=11a592fdc01846d4a1c728421295853d&_m=89b16abf81c642adb83db914d85d9f09&_e=u2n3XGDy_PyS-9ncrWxAAlAY4BP9Lf-1Pxuug8lLI-FfVMmLTUulb-HLtDyibWbhyFFRlduw5hvBBC5rGpRbGw5mFHEGdTtGN-e5-efAZz2lpxqh3Yx6HBesQHPqbD_M3L3OsTY_1m5c0iathPecmE_Rt-nHlHvZaTCsXJ8IRVx1AiMq7WXBEnjv3ChqwKKZMysL78fG7fsxlBPVa3TA3cdSdrYAuv_N-kULxRuttZE5_EPwmPswIr5sHZ0KtkSbfLFQ0q7eoSzOLLahVMr1s9-TCIFf9SqEOrlRFXpYEnVPydAD4K0TmitBvV-iZGn8_9SbskBQ4-K048TzNvULE-bhzeYk6zsxNvbCDMU19AO_RY8T1trG9UO6BGgHP9NTGAd6BtRI05A_cuZQYcV0Xhm_azOAsx_cKJCxzSpL52s%3D)
|
---
**Americans deserve the right to have the confidence and peace of mind that our elections have integrity.**
|
---
[ ](http://email.hawleyformo.com/u/click?_t=11a592fdc01846d4a1c728421295853d&_m=89b16abf81c642adb83db914d85d9f09&_e=u2n3XGDy_PyS-9ncrWxAAlAY4BP9Lf-1Pxuug8lLI-FfVMmLTUulb-HLtDyibWbhyFFRlduw5hvBBC5rGpRbGw5mFHEGdTtGN-e5-efAZz2lpxqh3Yx6HBesQHPqbD_M3L3OsTY_1m5c0iathPecmE_Rt-nHlHvZaTCsXJ8IRVx1AiMq7WXBEnjv3ChqwKKZMysL78fG7fsxlBPVa3TA3e_2XLyD5s1XBRJpdDB8fMwcjd5k7Jk3j-yuEgZLQCJOfLpfyQfztOJ3PgB_aWS4jPA-7-2wvBTSG7BW6qfKy06JOF5eLmonhIO1VyAiMRozgpaO40DZRpMgD0vjargVvHhRq7QyQPKyzoXG5nQ2yZvPrOKGinIxPDQcQRjmDdBN0nak6-muA3piivtZ7WH_3qdCqiShQIHjmGhAY7EsYug%3D)
|
---
_**We must ensure that our votes will count! **_

New election integrity laws, banning ballot harvesting, and guaranteeing that poll watchers will have appropriate access to actually do their jobs effectively are needed **_NOW_** to make our elections more transparent. _If you agree with me, please donate to help me and my team in this fight to bring much-needed improvements to our electoral process._
[**Donate _NOW_ to ensure American Election Integrity >>>**](http://email.hawleyformo.com/u/click?_t=11a592fdc01846d4a1c728421295853d&_m=89b16abf81c642adb83db914d85d9f09&_e=u2n3XGDy_PyS-9ncrWxAAlAY4BP9Lf-1Pxuug8lLI-FfVMmLTUulb-HLtDyibWbhyFFRlduw5hvBBC5rGpRbGw5mFHEGdTtGN-e5-efAZz2lpxqh3Yx6HBesQHPqbD_M3L3OsTY_1m5c0iathPecmE_Rt-nHlHvZaTCsXJ8IRVx1AiMq7WXBEnjv3ChqwKKZMysL78fG7fsxlBPVa3TA3W_geMHpC_EuCcJJaJ3TtuY3UOABUURN5SdMstIVLf4DCSRAm9_idPjvZYppxJnO9imxDGwOagG2BGklbT8AKV1CDZr6Fx00usogSMtPSQJXVpFiQJU7yeUNUUhcpU6dZY8Zc_uVzjj3dP1iu7fyeb1blUNiXRTpDMtE5XJ0qEj_P0Z4Rl0KCcxn9AUp57ix_wvkDSRKtyPR6B1-Q2mfS-g%3D)
Currently, **states control the voting process** , which includes counting and collecting ballots. It’s time we establish a system of accountability. This means allowing poll watchers from **BOTH sides of the aisle** to have access to oversee the vote counting.

_We must end ballot harvesting, by which you pay a third party to collect ballots. Common sense should tell us this shady practice ought to be illegal!_

**Join me to protect our electoral process through common sense reforms _TODAY_ so we can ensure our voice counts when we cast our ballot. **
[**Donate _HERE_**](http://email.hawleyformo.com/u/click?_t=11a592fdc01846d4a1c728421295853d&_m=89b16abf81c642adb83db914d85d9f09&_e=u2n3XGDy_PyS-9ncrWxAAlAY4BP9Lf-1Pxuug8lLI-FfVMmLTUulb-HLtDyibWbhyFFRlduw5hvBBC5rGpRbGw5mFHEGdTtGN-e5-efAZz2lpxqh3Yx6HBesQHPqbD_M3L3OsTY_1m5c0iathPecmE_Rt-nHlHvZaTCsXJ8IRVx1AiMq7WXBEnjv3ChqwKKZMysL78fG7fsxlBPVa3TA3c2FdI1pOPAEy7FWneNhlNH3Xv9GtgNb7Amm_M1NaatgBAPcn49vRFX_1WZkBzTlXxPNPg-L8jnE7_8LqF2G0IyqERo2hUW2A43tIRow-Km3awnFvgYKZCKz2d5Nl7O_6S2rEPmANjbi8Pg9VsT6kXax71etwVIzeNvO6XF4TpSN0G4OLv6vG61N92St6SEmleu-9Px4-Wh5RMJ5EZDJatY%3D)
Sincerely,

Josh Hawley
|

clean_body: | | Peter, If the 2020 elections made anything clear, it’s that greater accountability in our ballot-counting process is long past due. On Thursday night, I appeared on Fox News to discuss this critically important issue with Tucker Carlson. If you missed my interview, check it out by clicking on the image below. | Americans deserve the right to have the confidence and peace of mind that our elections have integrity. | | We must ensure that our votes will count! New election integrity laws, banning ballot harvesting, and guaranteeing that poll watchers will have appropriate access to actually do their jobs effectively are needed NOW to make our elections more transparent. If you agree with me, please donate to help me and my team in this fight to bring much-needed improvements to our electoral process. Currently, states control the voting process , which includes counting and collecting ballots. It’s time we establish a system of accountability. This means allowing poll watchers from BOTH sides of the aisle to have access to oversee the vote counting. We must end ballot harvesting, by which you pay a third party to collect ballots. Common sense should tell us this shady practice ought to be illegal! Join me to protect our electoral process through common sense reforms TODAY so we can ensure our voice counts when we cast our ballot. Sincerely, Josh Hawley | | | | | ---|---|---|--- Paid for by Josh Hawley for Senate

We have a number of recounts scheduled over the next couple of weeks, and your help will be crucial in ensuring we can oversee the process.

name: Unite the Country
subject: We did it!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Unite the Country, [www
body:
#
Peter --

We did it! Joe Biden will be the 46th President of the United States.

This announcement doesn't come without an immense amount of emotion for the Unite The Country team. It's been a long year but we've been fighting for Joe Biden since the beginning.

Joe Biden was made for this moment. He will be able to mend this country. As he frequently says, Joe Biden will be an American President.

Thank you for believing in us and, more importantly, believing in Joe Biden.

Our work isn't done. We will be sharing more in the weeks ahead but today, we should all celebrate the hard work that brought us here.

We will have a President with integrity, empathy and conviction. We should all bask in the excitement of this moment.

Mr. President-Elect, thank you for giving us hope again.

Thank you,

The Unite The Country Team

* * *
Paid for by Unite the Country, [www.unitethecountry.com](https://act.unitethecountry.com/page/m/46df773b/287b9173/2b04c38c/7e76422f/1814857923/VEsH/?g=x7_OMVH8x8VdxQ307JVTQtA "http://www.unitethecountry.com"), and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.
---
Unite the Country may not accept contributions from foreign nationals, federal contractors, national banks and corporations organized by authority of Congress. Federal law requires us to use our best efforts to collect and report the name, mailing address, occupation and employer of individuals whose contributions exceed $200 in a calendar year. Contributions to Unite the Country are not deductible as charitable contributions for federal income tax purposes.
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. We're counting on supporters like you, but if you want to unsubscribe, you can [click here](https://act.unitethecountry.com/page/m/46df773b/287b9173/2b04c38c/7e764228/1814857923/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/?g=x7_OMVH8x8VdxQ307JVTQtA "https://https://unitethecountry.cp.bsd.net/unsubscribe?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com").

Joe Biden and Kamala Harris have won the election.

The next chapter, though, is still being written. As votes continue to be counted, more of our fellow Democrats may take the lead in their races. Both Republicans and Democrats are preparing for recounts and lawsuits. And two runoffs will take place in Georgia in two short months.

The first step, though, is making sure that this election is fully resolved as quickly as possible. Then we must be ready to support every Democrat fighting their way through runoff elections to join us.

[**We have no time to waste. clean_body: "" Hey, Joe Biden and Kamala Harris have won the election. The next chapter, though, is still being written. As votes continue to be counted, more of our fellow Democrats may take the lead in their races. Both Republicans and Democrats are preparing for recounts and lawsuits. And two runoffs will take place in Georgia in two short months. Our work is not yet done, and we need you to keep fighting. "+" If you've saved payment info with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | "" "" "" "" I can't wait to work with Joe and Kamala to build our nation back better. Together, we'll ensure every American has access to healthcare, build an economy that works for everyone, and enact new legislation to ensure that our children and families can thrive. The first step, though, is making sure that this election is fully resolved as quickly as possible. Then we must be ready to support every Democrat fighting their way through runoff elections to join us. "" Thank you, Bob "" Paid for by Bob Casey for Senate Inc. PO Box 58746, Philadelphia, Pennsylvania 19102 Contributions or gifts to Bob Casey for Senate are not tax deductible.

name: Team Fair Fight Action
subject: Help Fair Fight Action contact voters!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Fair Fight Action
body:
|
---
[](https://secure.fairfightaction.com/page/m/41efa4e4/34a064cc/1a722f58/7022e0b/2759101329/VEsH/?g=yzW-IFH8x8VbBlZb5iRRXGA "https://fairfight.com/")

| | Friend - Election Day has passed, but our work is far from over. With a closely watched January 5 runoff election just around the corner, we need your help to reach Georgia voters in every corner of our state about making their plan to vote. **If you plan to vote by mail in the January runoff election, you can request your mail ballot online TODAY:[https://ballotrequest.sos.ga.gov/.](https://secure.fairfightaction.com/page/m/41efa4e4/34a064cc/1a722f58/7022e08/2759101329/VEsE/?g=yzW-IFH8x8VbBlZb5iRRXGA "https://ballotrequest.sos.ga.gov/") Don't wait - request your ballot now! Make your plan to vote today so that you can cast your ballot and have it count in the runoff election. ** After you've made your plan to vote, join us for our upcoming weekend of action and help us text and call hundreds of thousands of voters across Georgia about making their plan! With your help, we can contact voters about requesting their mail ballots and voting in the January runoff. **Runoff GOTV Weekend of Action
November 14 and 15
Virtual Phone and Text Bank
[SIGN UP HERE ](https://secure.fairfightaction.com/page/m/41efa4e4/34a064cc/1a722f58/7022e09/2759101329/VEsF/?g=yzW-IFH8x8VbBlZb5iRRXGA "https://www.mobilize.us/fairfightaction/event/362669/")** Directly contacting voters is a crucial way to ensure they have the information needed for this election. As we get ready for the January runoff, it's crucial that voters have a plan to cast their ballots and that they have the information they need to do so. Sign up today! Thank you for all you do, Team Fair Fight Action |
---|---|---

name: Team Evergreen
subject: Joe Biden ran on bold climate action -- and won.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY EVERGREEN ACTION
body:
A new era for our country, our economy, and our planet.
| [](https://act.evergreenaction.com/go/379?t=2&akid=4261%2E53494%2EnEuvDk) Friend, It thrills us to say it: On January 20, 2021, America will inaugurate its first climate president. That day will mark the beginning of a new era for our country, our economy, and our planet. Joe Biden ran on standards -- like 100% clean electricity by 2035. He ran on historic investments -- $2 trillion to build a clean-energy economy and create millions of good jobs. He ran on a commitment to justice -- prioritizing communities who have been hit first and worst by this crisis. There's no doubt about it: Joe Biden ran and won on bold climate action. To celebrate this historic victory, we put together a short video looking back on Joe Biden's bold climate advocacy on the 2020 campaign trail. **[Watch and share it with your friends as a reminder of the bold climate platform he ran (and won!) on!](https://act.evergreenaction.com/go/1165?t=3&akid=4261%2E53494%2EnEuvDk)** [](https://act.evergreenaction.com/go/1165?t=4&akid=4261%2E53494%2EnEuvDk) Throughout 2020, Biden and Trump offered starkly different approaches to the climate crisis. While Trump proclaimed his support for fossil fuel polluters, rolled back environmental protections, and abdicated American climate leadership on the international stage, Biden ran on a promise to tackle the climate crisis by investing in an equitable clean-energy economy, standing up for environmental justice, and creating millions of good-paying union jobs. We're thrilled that Biden's bold vision -- the result of months of listening to activists across the movement and working alongside former members of the Inslee, Warren, and Sanders campaigns -- ultimately won the day. **[Watch our video and share the news: America has elected its first climate president!](https://act.evergreenaction.com/go/1165?t=5&akid=4261%2E53494%2EnEuvDk)** | [SHARE ON FACEBOOK ](https://act.evergreenaction.com/go/1165?t=6&akid=4261%2E53494%2EnEuvDk)
---
[SHARE ON TWITTER ](https://act.evergreenaction.com/go/1166?t=7&akid=4261%2E53494%2EnEuvDk)
---
[SHARE ON INSTAGRAM ](https://act.evergreenaction.com/go/1167?t=8&akid=4261%2E53494%2EnEuvDk)
---
Thank you,
Team Evergreen

clean_body: A new era for our country, our economy, and our planet. | Friend, It thrills us to say it: On January 20, 2021, America will inaugurate its first climate president. That day will mark the beginning of a new era for our country, our economy, and our planet. Joe Biden ran on standards -- like 100% clean electricity by 2035. He ran on historic investments -- $2 trillion to build a clean-energy economy and create millions of good jobs. He ran on a commitment to justice -- prioritizing communities who have been hit first and worst by this crisis. There's no doubt about it: Joe Biden ran and won on bold climate action. To celebrate this historic victory, we put together a short video looking back on Joe Biden's bold climate advocacy on the 2020 campaign trail. Throughout 2020, Biden and Trump offered starkly different approaches to the climate crisis. While Trump proclaimed his support for fossil fuel polluters, rolled back environmental protections, and abdicated American climate leadership on the international stage, Biden ran on a promise to tackle the climate crisis by investing in an equitable clean-energy economy, standing up for environmental justice, and creating millions of good-paying union jobs. We're thrilled that Biden's bold vision -- the result of months of listening to activists across the movement and working alongside former members of the Inslee, Warren, and Sanders campaigns -- ultimately won the day. | Thank you, Team Evergreen

subject: NH Democrats already trying to reverse election results!

| Friends, **This is an urgent request. ** It's been less than 72 hours since New Hampshire voted to return the State Senate to a Republican majority and **the Democrats are already trying to overturn the results**! They have requested THREE recounts in districts that they lost by significant margins and have no realistic chance of overturning the results. In fact, in one district in which they have requested a recount, the Republican Senator won by nearly 900 votes! We need your help -- the recount process is expensive and we need the funds to make sure that it is done appropriately and fairly. [Can you help us by donating $5, $25 or even $100 to our "Recount Defense Fund?" Secure link to donate online here.](https://click.mlsend2.com/link/c/YT0xNTQ5NTE1NDc3NDEwMTI1MDUzJmM9ejdvNCZlPTE5MjgmYj00NDkxMDgyMTkmZD1nNGk3YTRu.4fvj-hdu6Be918VCazHfy5AflEIbJhXtRB8OxokTUgE) Read the statement by Senator Jeb Bradley:
---
---
| |
---
| |
---
| "It’s sad that Democrats are needlessly exposing many state employees and volunteers to Covid-19 as they pursue Senate recounts that will not change the outcome of the election given the margins of victory by Republicans in all three of the recounts.
These three recounts are nothing but a frivolous political ploy that ignores the very clear message from hard working voters that they truly don’t want an income tax or a sales tax. Apparently Democrats don’t want to accept this."
---
---
| |
---
| New Hampshire overwhelmingly voted for a Republican Majority, now we need to defend the will of the voters. Please consider making a small donation if you able.

Thank you,
The New Hampshire Senate Republicans
